mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically expanding equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals accelerating labor market tightness, which drives wage growth and consumer spending expectations, lifting equity earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or neutral 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicate broad-based demand expansion across housing and services sectors, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal instances show no statistically significant difference in 60-day forward returns compared to baseline (bootstrap p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%, inflationary pressure combined with restrictive policy compresses equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns in this dual-high regime are positive in > 60% of observations, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A positive three-month trend in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-month INDPRO trend shows no correlation with 5-day forward equity returns (Pearson r < 0.05) across multiple decades.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in retail sales exceeding 1.5% signals immediate weakening in consumer demand, triggering downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The threshold breach produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 50% of cases, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.1 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly payroll growth exceeds 200,000 while the unemployment rate simultaneously declines, the labor market exhibits a reinforcing expansion dynamic that boosts aggregate demand and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to outperform a random baseline in forward 60-day returns by at least 0.5% per month over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, a positive correlation between housing starts and real GDP growth signals capital expenditure acceleration and credit cycle normalization, which historically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and GDPC1 trend positively for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: The housing-GDP correlation signal produces negative forward returns in > 60% of instances during identified recovery periods, or predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS.
